talk_title: Spatial omics workflow and challenges for neuroimmune characterization
MOA_summary: Described the development and benchmarking of cell segmentation tools and hierarchical phenotyping pipelines for spatial transcriptomics and proteomics, emphasizing the importance of accurate nucleus and membrane segmentation to correctly assign immune phenotypes in tumor sections.
key_findings: Developed Circle Cell Segmentation Across Scales (CCSAS) tool that outperforms existing segmentation methods qualitatively and quantitatively.
Implemented a ranked hierarchy pixel‑by‑pixel classification approach for immune phenotyping that improves cell type assignment compared with simple dimensionality reduction.
Demonstrated that accurate segmentation dramatically impacts downstream spatial analyses such as pairwise cell interaction and cellular neighborhood quantification.
open_questions: How can segmentation algorithms be further optimized for heterogeneous tumor architectures and nerve‑rich regions?
What are the best practices for integrating multi‑modal spatial data (RNA, protein, metabolomics) in a single analysis pipeline?

talk_title: Spatial transcriptomics of perineural invasion in pancreatic cancer and PDGFD‑PDGFRB functional validation

speaker.name: Will Wang
speaker.affiliation: Massachusetts General Hospital / Harvard Medical School

targets.dependency_context: PDAC cancer cells adjacent to nerves
neurons, Schwann cells, PDAC cells near nerves

biomarkers.name: PDGFD protein
biomarkers.finding: Enriched in cancer cells proximal to nerves (p<0.01)

models.name: DRG‑Matrigel organoid co‑culture
models.genotype_or_key_features: tdTomato‑labeled PDAC organoids with CRISPR‑activated PDGFD or PDGFRB

clinical_data.value: significantly increased in PDGFD‑overexpressing orthotopic tumors

clinical_data.metric: neurite arborization length per cell body area

MOA_summary: Spatial transcriptomics identified PDGFD enrichment in cancer cells adjacent to nerves; functional assays showed PDGFD‑PDGFRB signaling promotes perineural invasion by enhancing cancer cell invasiveness, neurite outgrowth, and Schwann‑cell mediated tumor migration, which can be blocked by PDGFRB inhibition.
key_findings: A bulk RNA‑derived classifier predicts perineural invasion and uncovered missed PNI cases on histology.
Spatial profiling revealed PDGFD‑PDGFRB as the only ligand‑receptor pair overlapping between sequencing‑based (GeoMx) and imaging‑based (SMI) datasets.
CRISPR activation of PDGFD in PDAC organoids increased 3D neural invasion index, which was dose‑dependently blocked by the PDGFRB inhibitor SU16f.
Recombinant PDGFD stimulates neurite outgrowth from DRG neurons; this effect is abolished by PDGFRB inhibition.
In vivo, PDGFD overexpression augments orthotopic tumor nerve invasion, while PDGFRB inhibition reduces sciatic nerve invasion.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: PDGFRB inhibition selectively blocks PDGFD‑driven invasion without affecting baseline tumor growth in control lines.

combination_strategies.rationale: Block PDGFD‑PDGFRB signaling axis that drives perineural invasion.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Dose‑dependent reduction of nerve invasion in 3D assay and in vivo models.

open_questions: Will PDGFRB inhibition improve response to immunotherapy in PNI‑positive pancreatic cancer?
What are the downstream signaling pathways in neurons and Schwann cells mediating PDGFD effects?

talk_title: Integrating spatial neuroimmune signatures with bulk RNA to predict immunotherapy resistance

biomarkers.name: nerve injury signature (e.g., ATF3, C-JUN)
biomarkers.finding: Enriched in non‑responders to anti‑PD‑1 across multiple cohorts (p<0.01)

biomarkers.name: neurodegeneration GO terms
biomarkers.finding: High scores correlate with worse overall survival in TCGA head‑and‑neck cohort

models.name: Neoadjuvant anti‑PD‑1 trial (resectable skin cancer)
models.genotype_or_key_features: pre‑ and post‑treatment bulk RNA, spatial transcriptomics, proteomics

clinical_data.value: HR 0.56 for low nerve‑injury signature vs high

MOA_summary: Bulk RNA signatures of nerve injury and neurodegeneration are enriched in tumors that fail anti‑PD‑1 therapy; spatial profiling links these signatures to immunosuppressive microenvironments (e.g., FoxP3+ Tregs, myeloid cells) around injured nerves, suggesting nerve‑mediated resistance mechanisms.
readouts: IHC for NFH and immune markers
key_findings: Half of anti‑PD‑1 non‑responders exhibited histologic perineural invasion, whereas responders had significantly lower rates.
Bulk RNA‑seq of non‑responders showed enrichment of nerve injury pathways (ATF3, C‑JUN) and neurodegeneration GO terms.
Applying neurodegeneration signatures to TCGA head‑and‑neck cohort stratified patients by survival, with high‑signature tumors having worse outcomes.
Spatial profiling demonstrated co‑localization of nerve injury markers with immunosuppressive cells (FoxP3+ Tregs, myeloid cells) but not with effective anti‑tumor immune signatures.
NanoString GeoMx analysis confirmed that nerve‑adjacent regions in non‑responders express higher checkpoint molecules (PD‑L1, CTLA‑4) compared with responders.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Nerve injury signatures correlate specifically with immunosuppressive phenotypes, suggesting a selective resistance mechanism rather than a global tumor aggressiveness effect.

combination_strategies.rationale: Targeting nerve‑mediated immunosuppression may sensitize tumors to checkpoint inhibition.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Potential reduction of Treg enrichment around nerves and improved response rates (hypothetical).

combination_strategies.rationale: Block PDGFD‑PDGFRB axis implicated in nerve‑driven invasion and immunosuppression.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Reduced perineural invasion and decreased immunosuppressive niche in preclinical models.

open_questions: Can nerve‑injury signatures be used prospectively to stratify patients for combination neuro‑targeted therapies with immunotherapy?
What are the optimal biomarkers (RNA vs protein) for detecting nerve‑mediated immunosuppression in routine clinical samples?
